content:
Three top researchers walked out of Google in a single week. The obvious explanation is pre-IPO money. The real one is sitting in a footnote about who got the GPUs.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image by Ahmed M. AbdelfattahIn 2024, Google paid a reported $2.7 billion to bring one man back.
Noam Shazeer co-wrote the 2017 paper that invented the architecture every major AI model now runs on. He left Google in 2021 to start his own company, and was lured home in a deal large enough to make headlines.
He lasted less than twenty-two months. On June 18, he announced he was leaving again this time for OpenAI.
He was not the only one. In the same stretch, John Jumper, the Nobel laureate behind Google’s protein-folding breakthrough, left for Anthropic. Days later, Bloomberg reported that two more researchers central to Google’s Gemini model, Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel, were heading to Anthropic as well.
Three of the most important names in Google’s AI effort, out the door in roughly a week, all walking toward Google’s two biggest rivals.
The easy story writes itself: Anthropic and OpenAI are both about to go public, and a…
